description: Modern and contemporary biblical commentators and theologians

authors.name: Matthew Henry
authors.cultural_context: Post-Restoration England, Puritan tradition, Nonconformist movement
major_works: Matthew Henry's Commentary on the Whole Bible
authors.biography_summary: Son of nonconformist minister Philip Henry, Matthew Henry became one of the most influential biblical commentators in Protestant history. His commentary combines scholarly insight with practical devotional application.
authors.theological_contributions: Developed practical, devotional approach to biblical commentary that emphasized personal application and spiritual growth. His work bridges scholarly exegesis and pastoral care.
authors.historical_impact: His commentary became standard reference for Protestant preachers and teachers for centuries. Influenced countless ministers and shaped evangelical biblical interpretation.
authors.modern_significance: Still widely used for devotional study and preaching preparation. His practical approach to Scripture application remains influential in evangelical circles.
authors.scholarly_consensus: Recognized as one of the most influential biblical commentators in Protestant tradition, valued for practical insights despite being dated in critical methodology.
authors.portrait_description: 18th-century portrait of Matthew Henry as Puritan minister

authors.name: Charles Haddon Spurgeon
authors.cultural_context: Victorian England, Industrial Revolution, evangelical revival, Baptist denomination growth
authors.biography_summary: Known as the 'Prince of Preachers,' Spurgeon was the most famous preacher of the Victorian era. He pastored the Metropolitan Tabernacle in London and his sermons were published weekly worldwide.
authors.theological_contributions: Championed evangelical Reformed theology, emphasized personal relationship with Christ, developed practical approaches to Christian living, promoted missions and social action.
authors.historical_impact: His sermons reached millions through print media, influenced evangelical preaching worldwide, established orphanages and training colleges that continue today.
authors.modern_significance: His sermons and devotional works remain widely read. His preaching methods and theological insights continue to influence pastors and evangelists globally.
authors.scholarly_consensus: Universally recognized as one of history's greatest preachers, valued for spiritual insight and practical wisdom despite limited formal theological training.
authors.portrait_description: Formal portrait of C.H. Spurgeon as Victorian-era preacher

authors.name: John Calvin
authors.cultural_context: Protestant Reformation, Renaissance humanism, religious wars, development of Reformed theology
authors.biography_summary: French theologian and key figure of the Protestant Reformation, Calvin developed a comprehensive theological system that became foundational to Reformed Christianity. His influence extended beyond theology to politics and education.
authors.theological_contributions: Developed systematic Reformed theology including predestination, total depravity, sola scriptura, and comprehensive understanding of Christian life and society.
authors.historical_impact: Founded Reformed tradition that spread worldwide, influenced democratic political theory, established educational systems, shaped Protestant work ethic.
authors.modern_significance: Reformed theology continues to influence millions of Christians worldwide. His integration of faith and culture remains relevant for contemporary Christian thought.
authors.scholarly_consensus: Recognized as one of the most important theologians in Christian history, whose systematic approach and intellectual rigor shaped Protestant thought.
authors.portrait_description: Renaissance portrait of John Calvin as Reformer
authors.portrait_source: After Hans Holbein the Younger, 16th century. Wikimedia Commons
